comments:
Shape layer types and enums for vector graphics.

Shapes are the fundamental drawing primitives for motion graphics.
Shape layers can contain multiple shape contents including:
- Generators: Rectangle, Ellipse, Polygon, Star, Path
- Modifiers: Fill, Stroke, Gradient
- Operators: Trim, Merge, Offset, Wiggle, ZigZag

## Architecture

Mirrors `Lattice.Shapes` from the Haskell backend.

declarations.comments: Fill rule for determining inside/outside of paths.
declarations.title: FillRule

declarations.comments: Line cap style for stroke endpoints.
declarations.title: LineCap

declarations.comments: Line join style for stroke corners.
declarations.title: LineJoin

declarations.comments: Join type for Offset Paths operator.
declarations.title: OffsetJoin

declarations.comments: Point type for Wiggle Paths operator.
declarations.title: WigglePointType

declarations.comments:
Type of shape content item (26 variants).

Shape layers contain a stack of content items that are processed
in order to produce the final rendered shape.
declarations.title: ShapeContentType

declarations.comments: Check if shape content type is a generator.
declarations.title: isShapeContentTypeGenerator

declarations.comments: Check if shape content type is a modifier.
declarations.title: isShapeContentTypeModifier

declarations.comments: Check if shape content type is an operator.
declarations.title: isShapeContentTypeOperator

declarations.comments:
Convert path direction to integer multiplier.
Useful for winding calculations.
declarations.title: pathDirectionToInt

name: Hydrogen.Schema.Motion.Shapes
